name: CFSC Checks Cashed 2266 1st Ave
description_short: CFSC at 2266 1st Ave offers check cashing, money transfers, bill pay, and prepaid services in East Harlem with extended hours from 6am-10pm weekdays.
description_long: Community Financial Services Corporation (CFSC) operates a check-cashing location at 2266 1st Ave in East Harlem, Manhattan, serving the local community with financial services typically used by unbanked and underbanked consumers. The company provides traditional check-cashing services alongside a broader suite of financial and utility services designed to help customers manage money without requiring a traditional bank account. CFSC's primary offerings include check cashing, money transfers, money orders, electronic bill payment, prepaid debit cards, and ATM access, making it a one-stop financial services hub for routine transactions. The location distinguishes itself through extended operating hours (6am-10pm weekdays, 6am-8pm Saturdays, 7:30am-6pm Sundays), multilingual staff (Se Habla Español), and an unusually comprehensive service menu that extends beyond financial services to include UPS shipping, transit card services, lottery ticket sales, and phone services. CFSC operates as a network of 30+ locations across New York City and New Jersey, suggesting established operational infrastructure and brand recognition in urban markets. The honest assessment is that while CFSC provides genuine accessibility for consumers without bank accounts or those needing quick financial services, check-cashing companies typically charge fees that can be expensive relative to traditional banking, and the breadth of services doesn't imply specialized expertise in any single area. The company serves a real market need but operates in a sector that, by design, extracts costs from lower-income consumers.
pros: Exceptionally early opening hours (6am weekdays) accommodate shift workers and early-morning financial needs
Comprehensive service menu spanning financial, utility, and shipping services in one location reduces customer travel
Bilingual staff (Se Habla Español) serves non-English-speaking populations in diverse neighborhoods
Extended hours through 10pm weekdays allow access after traditional bank hours
ATM access on-site provides immediate cash without additional transaction fees
Established multi-location network (30+ locations) provides reliability and brand recognition
Foreign currency exchange service serves immigrant populations needing international transfers
cons: Check-cashing fees are not disclosed on the website, but industry standard rates typically range 1-5% and represent a recurring cost burden vs. free bank checking
No mention of financial counseling or guidance; the service model extracts value rather than builds financial capability
Limited transparency on money transfer fees, exchange rates, and prepaid card costs before customer visit
Reliance on cash-based services suggests limited integration with digital banking trends that might offer better rates elsewhere
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked workers needing reliable same-day check conversion in East Harlem
Immigrants and non-English speakers requiring foreign currency exchange and multilingual service
Shift workers and early-morning laborers who need financial services before traditional bank hours
diagnosis: CFSC 2266 1st Ave is best for unbanked or underbanked consumers in East Harlem needing quick check conversion or financial services outside traditional bank hours, particularly non-English speakers. The primary caveat is that check-cashing fees and money transfer costs—while not disclosed on the website—typically exceed what customers would pay at a bank or credit union, making this a convenience service that carries meaningful recurring costs.
